data.body:
RE-SCOPED TO idea + DETACHED FROM EPIC ace2 (2026-07-09, epic close-out evaluation). Rationale: (a) ace2's charter is reconciler CORRECTNESS + validation; its scope boundary explicitly routes maintainability/refactor work elsewhere, and eca4 (a pure module-loading refactor) is not referenced in the epic's ACs — it was appended by the 2026-07-08 audit. (b) The 2026-07-09 plan review returned BLOCK with 8 findings showing the true scope is far larger than the description (45 loader sites incl. the PRODUCTION __main__ entry; _engine is not a package; ~170 test files with their own inline loaders; 124 flat sys.modules pre-seedings). Per rebar semantics that is the definition of idea: captured but not designed. relates_to ace2 kept for provenance.

RECOMMENDED POSITIONS on the six open decisions (for the future design pass):
1. AC1 scope: define 'path-based loader' as ALL spec_from_file_location under src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ — including __main__.py _try_load_step/_load_sibling_keyed, pass_io.py, differ.py _load_sibling, inbound_translate.py. Grep target = spec_from_file_location, not _load(.
2. sys.modules key contract: replace the flat/dotted legacy keys with ONE canonical namespace (rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.<mod>). Package imports preserve both properties the contract exists for — stub injection (seed the canonical key) and class identity (normal import machinery guarantees one module object) — the bespoke key check becomes unnecessary, not unpreserved.
3. Test migration is unavoidably IN scope (finding 6 proves relative imports raise under the old loads): a shared conftest helper that imports the package normally + monkeypatch attribute-level stubbing replaces the 124 sys.modules pre-seedings; mechanical, scriptable sweep, but it is the bulk of the work.
4. Packaging: yes — create rebar/_engine/__init__.py (+ rebar_reconciler/__init__.py), include in wheel config; __main__ imports the package. This is the standard OSS pattern (plain package imports); the file-path loader was a workaround for the name shadowing, which the fully-qualified package name eliminates.
5. AC4: drop the IDE spot-check; fold into AC3 (make typecheck) — package imports make the modules visible to mypy/pyright by construction.
6. Scope-boundary coherence: the stated boundary ('no behavioral changes, no test-convention changes') is INCOMPATIBLE with the conversion — re-scope as its own small epic with 3 stories: (i) packaging + src conversion, (ii) test-tree migration, (iii) delete the loader + guards. Promote idea->open only after that redesign.

Sequencing note: deliberately parked until after the D1 harness + any in-flight reconciler work is quiet — it touches every reconciler module and has the biggest conflict surface (per the 2026-07-08 handoff: do LAST).
